commentaries.author: Hilary of Poitiers
commentaries.content: Jesus said unto them, Have you understood all these things. They say unto him, Yea, Lord. Hilary of Poitiers He spoke not to the crowds but to the disciples, and he gave fitting witness to those who understood the parables. He compared them with a householder, for they understood the teaching of his storeroom of things new and old. He referred to them as scribes because of their knowledge, for they understood the new and old things—that is, in the Gospels and in the law. He brought forth both of these on behalf of the same householder and from the same storeroom.

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Having then uttered all this, and concluded His discourse in a tone to cause fear, and signified that these are the majority of cases (for He dwelt more on them).
